items.text:
Subject: Welcome to the Fenwick Makerspace — Orientation Details

Dear Priya,

Welcome to the Fenwick Makerspace! We're excited you signed up for a membership this semester. Your orientation session is scheduled for Tuesday, September 15, from 4:00 PM to 5:30 PM in Room 118 of the Innovation Center.

Please bring your student ID to the orientation; you'll need it to activate your keycard access, which lets you enter the space anytime between 8:00 AM and 11:00 PM. Membership includes unlimited use of the 3D printers and laser cutters, though each printed item over 50 grams carries a $3 material fee, payable at the front desk or through the online portal.

A few reminders before you arrive:
- Closed-toe shoes are required whenever the laser cutter or table saw is in use.
- New members must complete a 15-minute safety quiz on the portal before touching any power tools.
- Lockers are available for $10 per semester if you would like to store personal projects on-site.

If you cannot make the September 15 session, email us to reserve a spot in the next one on September 22.

Best,
Marcus Ohl
Fenwick Makerspace Coordinator

format_metadata.subject: Welcome to the Fenwick Makerspace — Orientation Details

questions.stem: According to the email, what must Priya bring to the orientation session?

options.C: A printed copy of her class schedule

questions.explanation: The email tells Priya to bring her student ID to the orientation because she will need it to activate her keycard access.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about members who have not completed the safety quiz?

options.A: They will lose their membership entirely
options.B: They will be charged an additional material fee
options.C: They will need to reserve a locker instead
options.D: They will not be permitted to use power tools

questions.explanation: The email states new members must complete the safety quiz before touching any power tools, implying that those who have not finished it cannot use the equipment.

questions.stem: What is the main purpose of this email?

options.A: To welcome a new member and explain orientation details
options.B: To advertise a fundraiser for new equipment purchases
options.C: To request feedback about the previous semester's programming
options.D: To announce a temporary closure of the makerspace

questions.explanation: The email welcomes Priya as a new member and walks through the orientation time, access rules, fees, and safety requirements.

items.text:
@jordan.on.campus

Just got back from the Fall Maker Fair in the Quad and I'm still buzzing!! If you haven't been to one of these before, it's basically forty student clubs and local vendors setting up booths with free samples, live demos, and mini workshops.

I spent almost two hours at the pottery club's wheel-throwing station — they let you make your own mug for free, glaze included! There's also a $2 raffle table raising money for the robotics team's regional competition, and I bought six tickets because their prize table had an actual drone.

The fair runs through Saturday from 11 AM to 6 PM, so there's still time to catch it if you missed today. Who else is going tomorrow?? Drop a comment if you want to meet up at the pottery booth around noon.

#FallMakerFair #CampusLife

questions.stem: According to the post, what does the pottery club let visitors do for free?

options.A: To purchase a discounted glazing kit
options.B: To make their own mug at no cost
options.C: To sign up for a semester-long class
options.D: To watch a professional demonstration only

questions.explanation: The poster says the pottery club let visitors make their own mug for free, with glaze included.

questions.stem: The word "buzzing" in the first paragraph is closest in meaning to
questions.explanation: In context, "still buzzing" describes the poster's lingering excitement after attending the fair.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about the robotics team?

options.A: They will cancel their regional competition this year
options.B: They already have full funding from the university
options.C: They are raising money to attend a regional competition
options.D: They are selling mugs to fund their new drone

questions.explanation: The post mentions a raffle table raising money for the robotics team's regional competition, implying the team needs funds to attend it.
